title: NOC 72011 – Contractors and supervisors, electrical trades and telecommunications occupations | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Contractors and Supervisors (NOC 72011) are the operational backbone of Canada's digital and energy infrastructure. As the country pushes for a fully modernized electrical grid and expanded 5G/fiber-optic networks, these professionals are responsible for overseeing the critical work of electricians and telecommunications technicians. Unlike general management, this is a TEER 2 role that requires deep technical expertise—often gained as a Journeyperson—combined with leadership skills to manage multi-million dollar installation projects. In 2026, the role is heavily focused on Smart Grid integration and "Net-Zero" residential and industrial electrical upgrades.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Usually requires a college diploma, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group720 – Technical trades contractors and supervisors

duties: Project Coordination: Supervising and scheduling the activities of workers who install, repair, and maintain electrical wiring, fixtures, and telecommunication systems.
Operational Methods: Establishing methods to meet work schedules and coordinating activities with other subcontractors or departments.
Resource Management: Requisitioning materials and supplies and resolving work problems to improve productivity and quality.
Training & Safety: Training or arranging for the training of workers and ensuring that standards for safe working conditions (including 2026 Electrical Safety Codes) are strictly observed.
Reporting: Preparing work progress reports and recommending personnel actions such as hiring and promotions.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Experience: Several years of experience as a qualified Journeyperson Electrician or Telecommunications Technician are mandatory.
Certification: Journeyperson trade certification in a relevant electrical or telecommunications trade is required.
Licensing: In provinces like Ontario, an Electrical Contractor Licence from the Electrical Safety Authority (ESA) is required to perform or offer electrical work.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Strong, specifically driven by the National Housing Strategy and the federal commitment to rural high-speed internet:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In February and March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72011. Scores for the Foreign Worker stream draw in Southwestern and Northern Ontario started at 53–54, reflecting a high need for supervisors to manage the province's grid expansion.

British Columbia: Strong demand. Focused in the "Mainland/Southwest" region. BC projects over 1,400 openings in the construction and utilities sectors for this NOC through 2035, with a median wage of $45.00/hr.
Alberta: Strong demand. Managers here earn some of the highest wages, with an "Overall Average" of $45.26/hr and top earners reaching $60.50/hr.
National Trend: Over 90% of workers in this NOC are employed full-time. The replacement of retirees (currently 35%+ of the workforce is over 50) is the primary driver for international recruitment.
Why Work in Canada as an Electrical/Telecom Supervisor?
Lead the Digital Transition: Direct high-stakes projects involving 2026-grade technologies like IoT-connected electrical panels and high-density fiber-optic networks.
Top-Tier Trades Compensation: As a TEER 2 professional, your earning potential is significantly higher than general electricians, with average annual earnings reaching $98,000+.
Entrepreneurial Freedom: This NOC includes Contractors who own and operate their own firms, offering a path to business ownership in Canada's growing construction sector.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC remains a consistent target for Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) "Skilled Trades" draws.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On February 18 and March 25, 2026, OINP targeted this NOC under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a high-success route for those with a valid job offer in Ontario's utilities or construction sectors.
Express Entry (Federal Skilled Trades): As a TEER 2 technical trade, NOC 72011 is eligible for specialized "Trades" draws, which often have lower CRS requirements than general draws.

2026 TR to PR Pathway: A newly confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary workers. Supervisors in "Priority Infrastructure" sectors like telecommunications and electrical trades are top-tier candidates.
BC PNP Construction/Tech Streams: Highly technical supervisors (e.g., specialized telecommunications or industrial grid managers) are often targeted for BC's priority processing.
